pr.title: Mark some string symbols in gm/
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Updated GALE01 symbol metadata for the gm area by splitting several broad .data object ranges into smaller string-labeled symbols and tightening many .sdata string sizes. The PR was a config-only symbol annotation cleanup, improving data typing and object boundaries for game-mode/title-related data without touching C source.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, replaced several coarse object entries in the 0x803D8B88-0x803DA2E0 .data region with finer-grained labels marked as data:string.

Split previously large anonymous objects such as lbl_803D8B88 size 0xE8 into lbl_803D8B88 size 0x14 plus many adjacent string symbols including lbl_803D8B9C, lbl_803D8BA8, lbl_803D8BC4, lbl_803D8BF8, lbl_803D8C04, lbl_803D8C18, lbl_803D8C24, lbl_803D8C30, and lbl_803D8C40.

Adjusted existing string entries around lbl_803D8C70 through lbl_803D8CFC to more exact sizes and removed local scope markers from the excerpted entries where the new metadata no longer shows scope:local.
Split lbl_803D8D08 from size 0x540 to size 0x480 and added following string labels lbl_803D9188 through lbl_803D9224.
Split lbl_803D98C8 size 0x48 into four string symbols: lbl_803D98C8, lbl_803D98E4, lbl_803D98F0, and lbl_803D9904.
Split lbl_803DA040 size 0x90 and lbl_803DA254 size 0x8C into multiple adjacent data:string labels, leaving later aggregate blocks such as lbl_803DA0D0 and lbl_803DA2E0 intact.
Tightened many .sdata string sizes in the 0x804D4000-0x804D4220 region from padded 0x8 or 0xC declarations to shorter exact string lengths such as 0x7, 0x6, 0x5, or 0x3.
Preserved existing global gmTitle_804D4230 and gmTitle_804D4238 string labels immediately after the edited .sdata region.
smart_moves: Converted broad anonymous data blobs into individual string records rather than leaving strings hidden inside large object ranges, which should make later decompilation and reference analysis easier.

Used exact symbol boundaries and sizes for null-terminated strings instead of retaining padded slot sizes, especially in .sdata where many entries occupied 8-byte-aligned space but the actual string length was shorter.
Kept non-string aggregate blocks where appropriate, rather than over-splitting all surrounding data; examples include lbl_803D9248, lbl_803D9910, lbl_803DA0D0, and lbl_803DA2E0.
Maintained section/address continuity while inserting new labels, avoiding evidence of address movement or code changes.
decomp_lessons: When marking strings in symbols.txt, distinguish actual string byte length from alignment or padded storage range; the diff repeatedly changes size:0x8 strings to exact sizes like 0x5 or 0x7.
Large unnamed .data objects near jumptables may contain embedded string runs; splitting them into address-accurate data:string labels can clarify later disassembly and C data reconstruction.

A symbol metadata PR can materially improve decompilation context even without source changes, because typed string symbols improve searchability, data section interpretation, and likely generated assembly readability.
Do not infer meaningful names from string locations unless the PR provides evidence; this change mostly retained address-based lbl_ names and only marked type/size metadata.
naming_conventions: Kept default address-derived labels of the form lbl_803Dxxxx and lbl_804Dxxxx for newly identified strings when no semantic name was provided.
Kept existing jumptable_803Dxxxx names for jump tables and did not rename them while editing neighboring string data.
Retained existing gmTitle_804D4230 and gmTitle_804D4238 semantic global names; the PR did not introduce new semantic gm names in the provided excerpt.
Used symbols.txt metadata comments such as type:object, size:0xNN, data:string, data:byte, data:float, data:4byte, scope:local, and scope:global.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marking data:string in config/GALE01/symbols.txt is a non-code matching aid: it improves how data blobs are interpreted without changing compiled source.
Exact string sizing appears to account for terminators and padding separately, which helps prevent adjacent strings or non-string data from being swallowed into one object.
Splitting .data regions around jump tables and aggregate blocks can expose embedded string literals while preserving larger unknown objects for later analysis.
searchable_terms: Mark some string symbols in gm
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_803D8B9C through lbl_803D9224 to semantic gm string names.
Search for source files referencing the .sdata labels near lbl_804D4000 to determine which gm/title routines use these strings.
Check whether other GALE01 symbol regions still contain large object ranges with embedded unmarked strings.
Compare generated data dumps around 0x803D8B88 and 0x804D4000 to validate exact null-terminated string boundaries.
